metadata.filed_by: Arjun Deshmukh (via neighbor Mrs. Meena Kulkarni)

content:
FIRST INFORMATION REPORT

Under Section 154 CrPC, Police Station: Shivaji Nagar, Pune
FIR No.: FIR/2024/0087
Date of Registration: 12/03/2024
Time: 19:15 hrs

Complainant: Arjun Deshmukh, aged 42, resident of Shivaji Nagar, Pune, vegetable vendor. Complainant was physically assaulted and is currently admitted at Ruby Hall Clinic.

Factual Narrative:
On 12/03/2024 at approximately 18:35 hrs, the complainant was closing his vegetable stall outside Shree Ram Grocery Store, Shivaji Nagar, Pune. While turning to lock the gate, he was suddenly struck from behind on the left forearm and head with a heavy wooden plank by an unidentified male wearing a dark hoodie and blue jeans. The assailant fled towards the alley behind the store. The complainant fell unconscious and was found by Mrs. Meena Kulkarni, a neighbor, who alerted shop assistant Rajesh Patil. Both rushed the complainant to Ruby Hall Clinic, where he was diagnosed with a fractured left radius and multiple contusions.

CCTV footage from the grocery store entrance captured the incident. Three witnesses — Mrs. Meena Kulkarni, Rajesh Patil, and Vikram Joshi — identified the suspect as Rohan Deshmukh, estranged cousin of the complainant, due to a longstanding property dispute. A blood-stained wooden plank was recovered from behind the store, bearing fingerprints and blood matching the complainant. The suspect’s clothing and build match Rohan Deshmukh’s known appearance. The complainant has no known enemies other than Rohan Deshmukh. The case is being registered under IPC 323, 324, and 326.

Investigating Officer: SI Ramesh Desai
Signature: __________________

Date: 12/03/2024

metadata.relation_to_case: Neighbor of victim; maternal aunt of Rohan Deshmukh

content: I am Mrs. Meena Kulkarni, aged 58, residing at Flat No. 3, Shivaji Nagar. On 12/03/2024 at around 18:30 hrs, I was looking out my kitchen window to check if the rain had stopped. I saw a man in a dark blue hoodie and jeans standing behind Arjun Deshmukh. He raised a wooden plank and hit him hard on the arm. I saw his face clearly — it was Rohan Deshmukh. He looked angry, and I recognized him immediately. He ran towards the alley behind the store. I called out, but no one responded. I then ran to the shop and told Rajesh to help Arjun. I am certain it was Rohan. He has been threatening Arjun for months over the land dispute. I did not see anyone else near the scene.

metadata.relation_to_case: Shop assistant at Shree Ram Grocery Store

content: I am Rajesh Patil, 28, working at Shree Ram Grocery Store. On 12/03/2024, I was inside the store at 18:35 hrs, restocking packets. I heard a loud thud and looked out the front window. I saw a man in a bright blue hoodie hitting Arjun Deshmukh with a wooden plank. The man had a lean build and short hair — I’m sure it was Rohan Deshmukh. He ran off toward the railway station. I ran out and found Arjun unconscious. I called the police and helped carry him to the clinic. I told the constable the hoodie was blue, but later the report said dark grey. I think I was mistaken — I’ve seen Rohan wear blue before. I know him well — he comes here every Tuesday to buy onions.

I am Vikram Joshi, 34, a photographer. On 12/03/2024, I was walking from the bus stand to the railway station around 18:40 hrs. I saw a man in a dark hoodie running from the alley behind Shree Ram Grocery. He was holding something long — looked like a plank. He was running fast, looking back. I didn’t see his face, but he had the same build as Rohan Deshmukh, whom I’ve seen around the market. I thought he looked familiar. He turned right toward the bus stand, not the railway station. I told the constable that, but he said railway station made more sense, so I changed it. I didn’t want to cause trouble. I didn’t know who he was — just recognized the build.

metadata.source: Shree Ram Grocery Store Surveillance System

content:
CCTV FOOTAGE ANALYSIS REPORT

Evidence ID: E06
Source: Shree Ram Grocery Store — Entrance Camera (Camera ID: SRG-CAM01)

Timestamp Analysis:
- 18:22: Arjun Deshmukh locks stall gate, begins walking toward store entrance.
- 18:30: Man in dark grey hoodie (not blue) enters frame from alley behind store, walks slowly toward Arjun.
- 18:37: Man strikes Arjun from behind with wooden plank (visible in frame). Arjun collapses.
- 18:38: Man flees toward alley behind store, dropping plank.
- 18:42: Man exits alley, turns left toward rear street (not toward railway station).
- 18:45: Man disappears from view.

Findings:
1. Attacker’s hoodie is dark grey, not blue. No visible blue coloration.
2. Attacker’s face is partially obscured by hoodie and shadow. No clear facial recognition possible.
3. Attacker’s build: approximately 5'9", 70 kg, average build — matches Rohan Deshmukh but also others in area.
4. No other individuals visible in frame during attack.

Conclusion: The individual in the footage cannot be positively identified as Rohan Deshmukh based on facial features. Clothing color contradicts witness Rajesh Patil’s initial statement. The attack occurred at 18:37, not 18:30 as claimed by Mrs. Kulkarni. The window she claims to have looked out of faces north, while the attack occurred on the east side of the building — her view would be obstructed.

FORENSIC ANALYSIS REPORT — BLOOD-STAINED WOODEN PLANK

Evidence ID: E04
Recovered from alley behind Shree Ram Grocery Store, Shivaji Nagar, Pune on 12/03/2024.

1. DNA Analysis:
   - Blood sample matches Arjun Deshmukh (P01) with 99.98% probability.
   - No other human DNA detected.

2. Fingerprint Analysis:
   - Latent prints recovered: 3 partial prints.
   - One print matches Rohan Deshmukh (P02) — confirmed via AFIS.
   - Fingerprint analysis shows print was deposited on 10/03/2024 (verified via print age estimation and store purchase logs).
   - No recent prints (post-10/03) found.

3. Blood Pattern Analysis:
   - Blood spatter pattern consistent with blunt force trauma to forearm.
   - Blood amount indicates injury occurred at scene, not transported.

4. Additional Finding:
   - Blood on plank was not fresh at time of recovery — dried for approximately 12–18 hours.
   - Swabbing pattern suggests blood was deliberately applied using a swab from Ruby Hall Clinic’s medical records (matching protocol used in hospital for Arjun’s wound).

Conclusion: The wooden plank was used in the assault. However, Rohan Deshmukh’s fingerprints were not deposited during the assault. They were present from a prior visit. The blood was likely planted after the attack using a swab from the hospital. This indicates tampering with evidence.

DIGITAL FORENSICS ANALYSIS REPORT — FORGED WEDDING PHOTO

Evidence ID: E05
Submitted by Rohan Deshmukh as alibi evidence for 12/03/2024 wedding in Satara.

Image File: IMG_20240312_201523.jpg (JPEG)
Metadata:
- Created: 12/03/2024 20:15:23
- Modified: 12/03/2024 20:16:41
- GPS Coordinates: 17.6987° N, 75.9063° E (Satara Wedding Venue)
- Camera Model: Xiaomi Redmi Note 10 Pro

Analysis:
1. Image contains group photo of wedding guests. Face of suspect (Rohan Deshmukh) is digitally inserted.
2. Facial recognition analysis shows mismatch in skin tone, lighting angle, and shadow direction.
3. Background elements (e.g., lanterns, curtain folds) show inconsistent parallax — evidence of compositing.
4. Original photo (recovered from sister’s phone) was taken at 20:15 with 12 people. Rohan’s face was not among them.
5. Timestamp of photo (20:15) conflicts with Ramesh Kulkarni’s timeline: he was still en route to Satara via train, arriving at 21:30.
6. Phone usage logs show Ramesh Kulkarni borrowed a phone at 20:05 from a relative at Pune Railway Station — used to edit and send the photo.

Conclusion: The photo is a digitally altered composite. It was created to fabricate an alibi. The real perpetrator, Ramesh Kulkarni, used this photo to impersonate Rohan Deshmukh’s presence at the wedding.

INVESTIGATION REPORT — CASE NO. 005

Investigating Officer: SI Ramesh Desai, Shivaji Nagar PS
Date: 18/03/2024

Summary:
On 12/03/2024, Arjun Deshmukh was assaulted outside Shree Ram Grocery Store. Initial suspicion fell on Rohan Deshmukh due to familial dispute and witness identifications. However, forensic and digital evidence revealed inconsistencies.

Key Findings:
1. Witness statements contain contradictions:
   - Mrs. Meena Kulkarni claims to have seen the attacker’s face at 18:30, but CCTV shows attack at 18:37 and her window faces away from the scene.
   - Rajesh Patil initially stated ‘blue hoodie’ — later corrected to ‘dark grey’ after viewing CCTV.
   - Vikram Joshi changed his statement from ‘bus stand’ to ‘railway station’ under suggestive questioning.

2. Forensic Evidence:
   - Blood-stained plank contains Arjun’s blood and Rohan’s old fingerprints — no recent prints.
   - Blood was planted using hospital swab — evidence of tampering.

3. Digital Evidence:
   - Wedding photo presented as alibi is a forgery. Face of Rohan was digitally inserted into a photo taken at 20:15.
   - Ramesh Kulkarni (P03), brother-in-law of Mrs. Kulkarni, was not at the wedding at 20:15 — he was en route from Pune.

4. Motive:
   - Ramesh Kulkarni was reported to the excise department by Arjun on 28/02/2024 for illegal alcohol distillation. Excise records confirm complaint.
   - Ramesh Kulkarni traveled from Pimpri to Pune on rented scooter on 12/03/2024, then took a rickshaw to railway station, boarded train to Satara, arriving at 21:30 — just in time to be seen at reception.

5. Conclusion:
   - Rohan Deshmukh is not the perpetrator. His fingerprints and alibi are fabricated.
   - Mrs. Meena Kulkarni is the only witness who knowingly provided false testimony — she is Ramesh Kulkarni’s sister-in-law.
   - Ramesh Kulkarni is the actual perpetrator. He used the family feud to frame Rohan and fabricated an alibi using digital manipulation.

Recommendation: Charge sheet to be filed against Ramesh Kulkarni under IPC 324, 201 (tampering evidence), 193 (false evidence), and 420 (cheating). Mrs. Meena Kulkarni to be examined under Section 164 CrPC for perjury.

CHARGE SHEET

Court: Magistrate Court, Shivaji Nagar, Pune
Case No.: CRL.MC/2024/0112
Date: 20/03/2024
Accused: Ramesh Kulkarni, aged 45, resident of Pimpri, Pune

Charges under:
- Section 324 IPC: Voluntarily causing hurt by dangerous weapons
- Section 201 IPC: Causing disappearance of evidence
- Section 193 IPC: Giving false evidence
- Section 420 IPC: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
- Section 120B IPC: Criminal conspiracy (if accomplices identified later)

Facts:
On 12/03/2024, the accused, Ramesh Kulkarni, assaulted Arjun Deshmukh with a wooden plank outside Shree Ram Grocery Store, Shivaji Nagar, in retaliation for Arjun’s excise complaint filed on 28/02/2024. The accused then planted Rohan Deshmukh’s fingerprints on the weapon and used a forged wedding photo to establish a false alibi. Mrs. Meena Kulkarni, his sister-in-law, knowingly provided false testimony to implicate Rohan Deshmukh.

Evidence:
1. CCTV footage showing attacker’s build and route.
2. Forensic report confirming blood was planted from hospital swab.
3. Digital forensics report proving wedding photo is altered.
4. Train ticket and rickshaw receipt from Pune Railway Station (20:00–21:30).
5. Excise complaint record dated 28/02/2024.
6. Testimony of Vikram Joshi and Rajesh Patil (revised).

The accused is hereby charged and committed for trial.

Prosecutor: [Signature]
Date: 20/03/2024
